incorrects.description: Student selected 6, which is where the Soccer bar ends but ignores the scale of 2

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale axis emphasized.

steps.text: You counted where the bar ends. But look at the scale: each mark stands for 2 students, not 1. Count by 2s.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, close to correct but miscounted the intervals

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale markings emphasized.

steps.text: Almost. The scale goes by 2s. Count each mark carefully: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Where does the Soccer bar end?

incorrects.description: Student selected 24, which doubles the correct answer by counting each interval as 2 rather than each mark representing 2

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. Scale axis emphasized.

steps.text: That number is too high. The scale tells us what each mark means. Each mark on the axis shows 2 students. Count the marks where the Soccer bar ends.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Soccer bar highlights. System demonstrates counting by 2s along the scale.

steps.text: Let me show you. The scale goes by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The Soccer bar reaches the 12 mark. That means 12 students chose soccer. The answer is 12 because the scale tells us each mark represents 2 students, not 1.

incorrects.description: Student selected 18: added soccer and tennis instead of finding the difference

steps.text: You added soccer and tennis. The question asks how many more, which means we need to find the difference. Subtract the smaller number from the larger one.

incorrects.description: Student selected 12: only read soccer's value instead of comparing

steps.text: That's how many chose soccer. The question asks how many more chose soccer than tennis. Compare the two bars. What is the difference between their heights?

incorrects.description: Student selected 3: possibly subtracted 6 from 12 incorrectly or confused the values

steps.text: Not quite. Soccer has 12 students, tennis has 6. To find how many more, subtract. What is 12 minus 6?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight to demonstrate the comparison.

steps.text: Let me show you. How many more means we compare by finding the difference. Soccer is 12, tennis is 6. Subtract: 12 minus 6 equals 6. The answer is 6. That's what the difference between the two bars shows.

incorrects.description: Student selected 9: counted the marks instead of multiplying by scale

steps.text: You counted the marks on the bar. But look at the scale: each mark stands for 5 students. How many students is that?

incorrects.description: Student selected 25: read wrong category or miscounted

steps.text: That's the Tacos bar. We need Pizza. Count the marks on the Pizza bar and multiply by 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected 50: read axis maximum instead of bar height

steps.text: That's the top of the axis. Count where the Pizza bar stops. How many marks does it reach?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts marks on Pizza bar: 9 marks highlighted sequentially.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the marks on the Pizza bar: 9 marks. The scale says each mark equals 5 students. 9 times 5 equals 45. The answer is 45 students. When the scale isn't 1, you multiply the marks by the scale number.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

params.stem: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?
params.description: Word problem text appears: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: You've got graph reading down. You've got comparison down. But look at THIS question.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.
Word problem text: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.
Word problem text: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: See that word 'together'? This question needs more than one step. We'll come back to this question in the lesson, and you'll be able to answer it.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Lunch data. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.
Word problem text: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?
